description: The Republic of Ireland's Health Service Executive (HSE) has approved pembrolizumab 200mg in combination with carboplatin (AUC 5) and weekly paclitaxel 80mg/m^2 followed by doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide (AC 60/600) for reimbursement as a treatment option for the neoadjuvant treatment, and then pembrolizumab as a monotherapy treatment as an adjuvant treatment after surgery, of locally advanced or early stage triple negative breast cancer at high risk of recurrence in adult patients.

contributions.description: Adding HSE approvals for reimbursement to database

contributor.name: Van Allen lab
contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

reportedIn.name: Pembrolizumab 200mg, CARBOplatin AUC 5 and weekly PACLitaxel 80mg/m 2 followed by DOXOrubicin and cycloPHOSphamide (AC 60/600) Therapy. NCCP National SACT Regimen. HSE.

indication.indication: Pembrolizumab in combination with chemotherapy as neoadjuvant treatment, and then continued as monotherapy as adjuvant treatment after surgery, is indicated for the treatment of adults with locally advanced, or early stage triple negative breast cancer at high risk of recurrence.
indication.description: The Republic of Ireland's Health Service Executive (HSE) has approved pembrolizumab 200mg in combination with carboplatin (AUC 5) and weekly paclitaxel 80mg/m^2 followed by doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide (AC 60/600) for reimbursement as a treatment option for the neoadjuvant treatment, and then pembrolizumab as a monotherapy treatment as an adjuvant treatment after surgery, of locally advanced or early stage triple negative breast cancer at high risk of recurrence in adult patients.
indication.raw_therapeutics: Pembrolizumab (200mg), carboplatin AUC 5 and weekly paclitaxel 80mg/m^2 followed by doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide (ac 60/600) therapy.
